blocks:
Sūrat Idhā Zulzilat

And it is named Sūrat al-Zilzāl. It is Medinan (مدنيّة) according to Ibn ‘Abbās and Qatādah, and Meccan (مكيّة) according to al-Ḍaḥḥāk and ‘Aṭā’. The number of its verses (آيها) is eight verses (آيات) according to the Kūfan (الكوفي) and the First Medinan (المدني الأوّل) counts, and nine in the remaining counts. The point of difference (اختلافها) is the verse أَشْتاتاً ** (in the counts) other than the Kūfan and the First Medinan.* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) إِذا زُلْزِلَتِ الْأَرْضُ زِلْزالَها ** (1) When the earth is shaken with its [final] shaking, Its tremor/agitation (اضطرابها).

(٢) وَأَخْرَجَتِ الْأَرْضُ أَثْقالَها ** (2) and the earth casts out its burdens, From the buried treasures (الدفاين) and the dead (الأموات). (أثقالها) is the plural of (ثقل), which is the household goods (متاع البيت). And al-Qummi said: [It refers to] the people (من الناس).

(٣)** وَقالَ الْإِنْسانُ ما لَها ** (3) and man says, 'What is the matter with it?' He said: That [man] is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(٤) يَوْمَئِذٍ تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبارَها. (4) On that day she will relate her news, In al-Kharā’ij, narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): This Sūrah was recited in the presence of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and he said: "I am الْإِنْسانُ ** (the man), and it is to me that تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبارَها.** (she will relate her news)."

And in al-’Ilal, narrated from Tamīm ibn Ḥātim, who said: We were with ‘Alī (peace be upon him) when we were heading towards Basra. He said: While we were resting, the earth trembled. ‘Alī (peace be upon him) struck it with his noble hand and said to it: "What is the matter with you (ما لك)?" Then He turned to us with His noble face and said to us: "Know that if this were the earthquake that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, mentioned in His Glorious Book, it would have answered Me, but this is not that [earthquake]."

And in al-Kāfī: [A narration] with the same meaning (ما في معناه).

And in al-’Ilal, narrated from Fāṭimah (peace be upon her), who said: An earthquake struck the people during the time of Abū Bakr. The people rushed in fear to Abū Bakr and ‘Umar, and they found that both of them had gone out in fear to ‘Alī (peace be upon him). The people followed them until they reached the door of ‘Alī (peace be upon him). He came out to them, unconcerned by their state of fear. He proceeded, and the people followed Him until they reached a hill (تلعة). He sat upon it, and they sat around Him, watching the walls of the city shaking, coming and going. ‘Alī (peace be upon him) said to them: "It seems that what you see has terrified you." They said: "How can it not terrify us, when we have never seen anything like it?" She said: He moved His lips, then struck the earth with His noble hand, and then said: "What is the matter with you (ما لك)? Be still (اسكني)." So, it became still by the permission of Allah. They were more astonished by this than they were by their initial astonishment when He came out to them. He said to them: "Have you been astonished by My action?" They said: "Yes." He said: "I am the man (الرجل) concerning whom Allah said: ** إِذا زُلْزِلَتِ الْأَرْضُ زِلْزالَها وَأَخْرَجَتِ الْأَرْضُ أَثْقالَها وَقالَ الْإِنْسانُ ما لَها ** (When the earth is shaken with its [final] shaking, and the earth casts out its burdens, and man says, 'What is the matter with it?'). I am the man (الْإِنْسانُ) who says to it: 'What is the matter with you (ما لك)?' يَوْمَئِذٍ تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبارَها (On that day she will relate her news)—it relates [its news] to Me."

And in al-Majma’, it is narrated in the Ḥadīth: The Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) said: "Do you know what أَخْبارَها (her news) is?" They said: "Allah and His Messenger know best." He said: "Her news is that she will bear witness against every male and female servant concerning what they did upon her surface. She will say: 'He did such-and-such, and she did such-and-such.' This is her news."

(٥) بِأَنَّ رَبَّكَ أَوْحى لَها ** (5) for your Lord will have inspired it. Meaning, it speaks because of the inspiration of your Lord to it, or by the inspiration of your Lord to it. (٦) يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصْدُرُ النَّاسُ (١) (6) On that day people will emerge (1) those who are in the graves to the Standing (Place of Judgment), scattered (أَشْتاتاً), dispersed according to their ranks. Al-Qummi said: They will come scattered (أَشْتاتاً): believers, disbelievers, and hypocrites. لِيُرَوْا أَعْمالَهُمْ ** to be shown their deeds. He said: So that they may stand upon what they did.

(٧) فَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْراً يَرَهُ. (7) Whoever does an atom’s weight of good will see it. (٨) وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ شَرًّا يَرَهُ (8) And whoever does an atom’s weight of evil will see it. And it was recited (يُره) with the ḍammah on the yā’ in both (verses). And he narrated it in al-Majma‘ from Ali (peace be upon him). It was said that this is the most decisive (أحكم) verse in the Quran, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to call it al-Jāmi‘ah (the Comprehensive).

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: فَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْراً يَرَهُ ** (7) Whoever does an atom’s weight of good will see it. He said: It means that if he is among the people of the Fire (أهل النّار) and had done an atom's weight of good in the world (الدنيا), he will see it on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) as regret (حسرة) because his deed was done for other than Allah. وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ شَرًّا يَرَهُ ** (8) And whoever does an atom’s weight of evil will see it. He said: It means that if he is among the people of Paradise (أهل الجنّة) and committed evil, he will see that evil on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), and then He will forgive him.

In Thawāb al-A‘māl and al-Majma‘, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Do not tire of reciting إذا زلزلت الأرض (When the earth is shaken), for whoever makes its recitation part of his supererogatory prayers (نوافله), Allah will never afflict him with an earthquake, nor will he die by it, nor by a thunderbolt (صاعقة), nor by any affliction (آفة) from the afflictions of the world (الدنيا). When he dies, he will be commanded to enter Paradise (الجنّة). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will say: "My servant, I have made My Paradise lawful for you, so dwell therein wherever you wish and desire, neither forbidden nor repelled." And in al-Kāfī there is that which conveys the same meaning, with additions (زيادات).

footnote (١) Meaning: The people will return from the station of reckoning (*موقف الحساب*) after the presentation (*العرض*), separated: the people of faith (*أهل الإيمان*) distinctly, and the people of every (other) religion distinctly.
